title: Ceph Cluster Health Degraded / HEALTH_WARN or HEALTH_ERR
symptoms: ceph status shows HEALTH_WARN or HEALTH_ERR
CephCluster CR shows Phase=Ready but Health=HEALTH_WARN
Alert: CephClusterErrorState or CephClusterWarningState firing
Persistent Volume claims stuck in Pending
causes: One or more OSDs down or out
MON quorum issues (lost majority of monitors)
Pool PG degraded or undersized
Slow OSD ops / high latency
Clock skew between nodes (>0.05s triggers HEALTH_WARN)
investigation: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph status
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph health detail
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd tree
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd df
remediation: If OSD is down: kubectl -n rook-ceph delete pod <osd-pod> to trigger restart
If OSD is out: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd in <id>
For clock skew: check chronyc tracking on all nodes via SSH, restart chronyd
For PG issues: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph pg repair <pgid>
For slow ops: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd blocked-by
If operator stuck: kubectl -n rook-ceph rollout restart deploy/rook-ceph-operator
escalation: If HEALTH_ERR persists >10min or data loss suspected, escalate to storage team. Do NOT delete OSDs or PGs without approval.

title: Ceph OSD Down / OSD Not Running
symptoms: Alert: CephOSDDown firing
kubectl -n rook-ceph get pods shows osd pod in CrashLoopBackOff or Error
ceph osd tree shows OSD as DOWN
PVC attach failures for rook-ceph-block storage class
causes: Underlying disk failure or I/O error
Node the OSD runs on is unreachable
OSD crashed due to memory pressure (OOMKilled)
Incorrect disk permissions or bluestore corruption
PodAntiAffinity conflict after node rescheduling
investigation: kubectl -n rook-ceph get pods -l app=rook-ceph-osd -o wide
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd tree
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd status
# On the node (via SSH): dmesg | grep -i 'I/O error\|blk\|nvme' | tail -50
remediation: Delete crashed OSD pod to let it restart: kubectl -n rook-ceph delete pod <osd-pod>
If OSD stays down after restart, check node disk health via SSH
For OOMKilled: increase OSD memory limit in CephCluster CR spec.storage.config
For persistent disk I/O errors: mark disk as failed, replace hardware
After disk replacement: new OSD will auto-provision via rook-ceph-operator
Monitor rebalancing: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph -w
escalation: Hardware disk failure requires physical replacement. Escalate if more than 1 OSD is down simultaneously — risk of data loss if pool min_size is breached.

title: Ceph MON Quorum Lost / Monitor Not In Quorum
symptoms: Alert: CephMonQuorumAtRisk or CephMonQuorumLost
ceph status shows 'mons X,Y are out of quorum'
All Ceph operations blocked / hung
rook-ceph-tools exec hangs indefinitely
causes: Majority of MON pods down (need >50% for quorum)
Network partition between MON nodes
Clock skew causing election failures
MON store corruption
investigation: # Check if >1 mon is running. With 3 mons you need at least 2 up.
# If stuck: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it <working-mon-pod> -- ceph mon stat
remediation: Restart down MON pods: kubectl -n rook-ceph delete pod <mon-pod>
If node is down: cordon/drain is NOT needed, wait for node to recover
For clock skew: SSH to each node, run 'chronyc tracking' and fix NTP sync
For store corruption: follow Rook disaster recovery docs (requires operator intervention)
If <2 mons available: this is a cluster outage — escalate immediately
escalation: MON quorum loss with <2 mons running is a critical storage outage. Escalate to storage team with urgency. Do not attempt MON store surgery without expertise.

title: Ceph Storage Pool Nearly Full / OSD Near Full
symptoms: Alert: CephPoolNearFull or CephOSDNearFull firing
ceph df shows usage >75%
New PVC creation fails with 'no space left on device'
ceph status shows HEALTH_WARN 'X nearfull osds'
causes: Rapid PVC growth or large workload data increase
Replication overhead (3x for replicated pools)
Deleted data not yet freed (scrub/compaction lag)
Cluster was undersized for workload
investigation: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph df detail
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph osd df tree
# Identify largest PVCs and their consumers
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- rados df
remediation: Immediate: free space by deleting unused PVCs: kubectl delete pvc <name> -n <ns>
Short term: expand existing storage nodes (add disks or replace with larger ones)
Add new storage nodes to cluster — Rook will auto-rebalance
If pool full (>85%): emergency — disable nearfull ratio temporarily in CephCluster CR
Reduce replication factor only as last resort (impacts data safety)
escalation: If usage exceeds 85%, Ceph enters read-only mode. This is a P1 storage incident. Alert storage team immediately.

title: Rook-Ceph CSI Driver / RBD PVC Attach Failure
symptoms: Pod stuck in ContainerCreating with 'Unable to attach or mount volumes'
kubectl describe pod shows 'rbd: map failed exit status 5'
PVC in Bound state but pod cannot start
csi-rbdplugin pod in error state
causes: RBD kernel module not loaded on worker node
CSI provisioner/attacher pod crashed
Ceph cluster health degraded (CSI blocks on unhealthy cluster)
Node selector conflict — pod scheduled to node without RBD access
Stale volumeattachment object from previous failed attach
investigation: # On node (SSH): dmesg | grep rbd | tail -20
remediation: Load RBD module on node: SSH to node, run 'modprobe rbd'
Make persistent: SSH to node, add 'rbd' to /etc/modules-load.d/rbd.conf
Restart CSI pods: kubectl -n rook-ceph rollout restart ds/csi-rbdplugin
Delete stale VolumeAttachment: kubectl delete volumeattachment <name>
If CSI provisioner crashed: kubectl -n rook-ceph rollout restart deploy/csi-rbdplugin-provisioner
For Flatcar: CSI driver may need specific kernel version — check compatibility
escalation: If multiple nodes cannot attach RBD volumes, this affects all storage-backed workloads. Escalate to platform team.

title: Rook-Ceph CephFilesystem / CephFS Subvolume Issues
symptoms: RWX PVC stuck in Pending
Pod using CephFS mount shows 'mount: wrong fs type' error
MDS pods in CrashLoopBackOff
Alert: CephMdsMissingReplicas
causes: MDS daemon not running or crashed
CephFS kernel module missing on node
CephFilesystem CR not ready
Subvolume group not created
investigation: kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph fs status
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec deploy/rook-ceph-tools -- ceph fs ls
remediation: Restart MDS pods: kubectl -n rook-ceph delete pod -l app=rook-ceph-mds
Load kernel module on node: SSH to node, 'modprobe ceph'
Verify CephFilesystem CR is healthy: kubectl -n rook-ceph get cephfilesystem
Check csi-cephfsplugin daemonset: kubectl -n rook-ceph rollout restart ds/csi-cephfsplugin
escalation: RWX PVC failures affect stateful workloads needing shared storage. Escalate if MDS stays crashed after restart.
